name: Credit.Org - Seattle Branch
description_short: Credit.Org is a nonprofit credit counseling and housing assistance organization offering free debt relief, credit building, and HUD-approved mortgage counseling since 1974.
description_long:
Credit.Org is a national nonprofit organization that has provided financial counseling and housing assistance for over 50 years, completing more than 5.2 million counseling sessions and delivering $2.2 billion in homeowner support. The organization operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency with certified nonprofit counselors available nationwide, including a Seattle branch. Their business model is entirely based on providing free or low-cost financial guidance rather than selling financial products or services directly to consumers.

The organization offers comprehensive counseling across six primary service areas: debt relief and management, credit building and repair, housing assistance (including reverse mortgages, foreclosure prevention, and pre-purchase counseling), rental readiness support, disaster recovery financial planning, and bankruptcy option education. All services are delivered through one-on-one personalized counseling sessions with certified advisors, plus self-paced educational content through their FIT Academy, live webinar classes, and financial calculators. Clients work through a three-step process: an initial free consultation to assess goals, development of a personalized financial plan, and ongoing support to implement and reach objectives.

Credit.Org distinguishes itself through its nonprofit structure, extensive partner network, multi-language support (English and Spanish), and dual focus on both immediate financial relief and long-term financial wellness. The organization emphasizes education and personal agency rather than selling proprietary debt relief or credit products. They operate specialized programs like reverse mortgage counseling (both HECM and non-HECM), workplace financial wellness events, and disaster recovery assistance. The website features client testimonials and highlights their HUD approval status, which carries significant credibility in the housing counseling space.

While Credit.Org's nonprofit status and free counseling model are genuinely valuable, potential clients should understand that the organization does not directly provide loans, credit cards, or debt settlement services—they educate clients and connect them with appropriate resources. The depth and quality of personalized assistance may vary based on counselor availability and the complexity of individual financial situations. For consumers seeking hands-on debt settlement negotiation or immediate credit repair, this organization's advisory approach may require more personal initiative than direct service providers.
pros: Truly free counseling sessions with no hidden fees or product sales requirements
HUD-approved housing counselors with required certifications and 50+ years of organizational track record
Comprehensive service range covering debt, credit, housing, rental, bankruptcy, and disaster recovery in one organization
Bilingual services in English and Spanish across multiple service areas
Self-paced FIT Academy courses, live interactive webinars, and calculators complement personalized counseling
Verified scale: 5.2+ million counseling sessions completed and $2.2 billion in homeowner support delivered
Specialized reverse mortgage counseling (both required HECM and non-HECM tracks) with documented client satisfaction
cons: Organization provides counseling and guidance only—does not directly provide loans, debt settlement, or credit repair services
Clients must take personal action to implement recommendations; organization does not negotiate with creditors on their behalf
Quality and availability of personalized counseling likely varies by location and counselor caseload
Website does not clearly explain wait times for appointments or expected timeframe for receiving personalized financial plans
No mention of financial outcome guarantees or performance metrics for debt reduction, credit score improvement, or housing success rates
best_for: Homeowners and renters seeking free HUD-approved housing counseling without product sales pressure
Consumers looking for comprehensive financial education alongside personalized debt or credit guidance
Spanish-speaking individuals needing multilingual financial counseling and education resources
Homeowners exploring reverse mortgages who need unbiased, required counseling before committing to the loan
diagnosis: Credit.Org is best for consumers who want free, nonprofit financial counseling and education without pressure to buy products, particularly those facing housing decisions, significant debt, or credit challenges. The main caveat is that this organization educates and advises—it does not directly settle debts, dispute credit reports, or provide loans, so clients must be prepared to take independent action based on their personalized plan.
services: Free personalized debt relief and debt management counseling sessions
Credit building, credit score improvement, and credit report review counseling
HUD-approved pre-purchase homeownership preparation and education
Reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and non-HECM programs)
Foreclosure prevention and mortgage default assistance
Rental readiness coaching and rental assistance guidance
Disaster recovery financial planning and support
Self-paced FIT Academy online courses on budgeting, debt, credit, and homeownership
Live interactive webinar classes with financial experts
Financial planning calculators for budgeting, debt paydown, and retirement
Workplace financial wellness events and employee education programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 450 Alaskan Way S, Suite 200, Seattle, WA 98104
